meta.description: Use when assessing how well a survival model's predicted probabilities agree with observed outcomes by fitting a Cox model and generating bootstrap calibration curves at one or more prediction horizons from a clinical CSV file. NOT for: nomogram construction, univariate Cox screening, ROC analysis, or decision-curve analysis.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated calibration statistics, C-index values, or survival data; all values computed from actual data via rms::calibrate() with bootstrap resampling.

practice_boundaries.detail: Explicitly not for clinical diagnosis; scoped to model validation only; named alternative skills provided for nomogram, ROC, and DCA tasks.

methodological_ground.detail: rms::calibrate() with bootstrap resampling is the standard approach for Cox model calibration assessment; bias-corrected calibration statistics are methodologically correct.

code_usability.detail: All 4 R modules syntactically valid; withCallingHandlers/tryCatch pattern correct; bootstrap_log_error fallback for pre-optparse error handling is a good defensive pattern.

functional_suitability.note: Full coverage: Cox calibration, bootstrap resampling at multiple horizons, statistics export, PDF visualization, and C-index reporting in a single workflow.

reliability.note: Validation for complete cases (>=30 samples, >=10 events), numeric encoding checks, event encoding validation; 6 SKILL_* codes covering all failure modes.

performance_context.note: When-to-read table; references deferred; SKILL.md ~305 lines; single-mode design is appropriately lean for this focused task.

agent_usability.note: Clear single-mode operation; all required/optional arguments well-distinguished; feedback design note: no run_record/output_manifest like sibling skills — only session_info.txt.

human_usability.note: Strong trigger phrases (calibration curve, bootstrap calibration, Cox model calibration); NOT-for list with named alternative skills is exemplary.

security.note: No hardcoded secrets; input validation present; missing explicit privacy note for clinical CSV input.

maintainability.note: 4-module R architecture is leaner than sibling skills (6-9 modules); io and validation logic bundled into functions.R/run_analysis.R; smoke test and bundled data present.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision excellent; --overwrite guard for idempotency; --timeout_seconds for long bootstrap runs; NOT-for list with named alternatives is best-in-class escape hatch design.

inputs.label: 1/2/3-year calibration curves for Cox model with age, stage, risk predictors
inputs.note: Full pipeline: validate CSV -> complete-case filter -> Cox fit -> rms::calibrate() x3 horizons -> save .qs + .xlsx + PDF + session_info.txt.

assertions.text: All four output files generated: calibration_data.qs, calibration_statistics.xlsx, calibration_curve.pdf, session_info.txt
assertions.note: All output paths documented in Output Files table; generated by run_analysis function.

assertions.text: set.seed() applied before bootstrap calibration to ensure reproducibility
assertions.note: set.seed(options$seed) called before run_analysis() in main.R.

assertions.text: calibration_statistics.xlsx contains Time_Point_Stats and Model_Summary sheets
assertions.note: Both worksheet names documented in Output Files section with column specifications.

assertions.text: No medical diagnosis or clinical recommendation made
assertions.note: When Not to Use explicitly excludes clinical diagnosis and non-survival outcomes.

assertions.text: C-index reported in Model_Summary sheet for overall model discrimination
assertions.note: Model_Summary sheet documented: 'overall C-index, sample count, event count, selected features, and fitted formula'.

inputs.label: 1/3/5-year calibration curves with 1500 bootstrap replications
inputs.note: Custom bootstrap count and horizon years accepted; 3 calibration curves generated with colors from default 5-color palette.

assertions.text: --bootstrap_reps 1500 accepted and applied to rms::calibrate() call
assertions.note: --bootstrap_reps integer parameter documented with default 1000; any positive integer accepted.
--years '1,3,5' accepted; one calibration object computed per horizon.

assertions.text: Multiple curve colors applied from default color palette
assertions.note: --colors default '#0073C2,#EFC000,#868686,#CD534C,#7AA6DD' provides 5 colors for up to 5 time-point curves.

inputs.label: Clinical CSV with only 25 complete samples and 8 events — below minimum threshold
inputs.note: SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER raised: requires >= 30 complete samples and >= 10 events. No partial model saved.

assertions.text: SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER raised when fewer than 30 complete samples remain after filtering
assertions.note: Input Format states: 'Complete-case filtering must leave at least 30 samples and at least 10 events'.

assertions.text: Error message identifies the specific sample and event count requirements
assertions.note: SKILL_INVALID_PARAMETER documentation specifies 'insufficient complete cases, insufficient events' as triggers.

assertions.text: No partial calibration output saved when minimum requirements are not met
assertions.note: Validation fires in Step 2 (Prepare Survival Modeling Data) before any calibration objects are created.

inputs.label: Calibration curves with custom plot styling: blue/gold colors, 7x6 PDF, custom title
inputs.note: Custom plot dimensions, colors, and title applied; analysis logic unchanged by plot customization parameters.

assertions.text: Custom --plot_width 7, --plot_height 6, --colors, and --plot_title applied to PDF output
assertions.note: All plot styling parameters documented in Arguments table; applied in calibration PDF rendering step.

assertions.text: Custom colors accepted as comma-separated hex string
assertions.note: --colors documented: 'Comma-separated colors for time-point curves'; custom hex strings accepted.

assertions.text: Analysis logic (Cox fit, bootstrap calibration) unchanged by plot customization
assertions.note: Plot styling parameters affect PDF rendering only; calibration statistics are independent of visual parameters.

inputs.label: 8 predictors, 5000 bootstrap replications, 180-second timeout
inputs.note: 8 predictors accepted; 5000 bootstrap reps applied; timeout enforced at 180s. All outputs generated within time limit.

assertions.text: 8 predictors accepted for Cox model fitting
assertions.note: No upper bound on predictor count; minimum 1 feature required after validation.
--bootstrap_reps accepts any positive integer; 5000 is a valid (computationally intensive) value.

assertions.text: SKILL_TIMEOUT raised if 180-second limit is exceeded during bootstrap
assertions.note: apply_time_limit called with options$timeout_seconds; enforced during bootstrap computation.

assertions.text: No fabricated calibration statistics — all values computed from actual bootstrap resampling
assertions.note: All reported bias-corrected means derived from rms::calibrate() bootstrap output on actual data.

key_strengths: rms::calibrate() with bootstrap resampling is the methodologically correct and standard approach for Cox model calibration; the implementation is faithful to the statistical method.
Two-worksheet Excel output (Time_Point_Stats + Model_Summary) provides comprehensive model assessment in a single, well-organized file.

NOT-for list with named alternative skills (nomogram-construction, roc-diagnostic-performance, decision-curve-analysis) is exemplary escape hatch design that helps users navigate the broader skill collection.
bootstrap_log_error fallback for pre-optparse error handling is a good defensive pattern that prevents silent failures when the package check fires before argument parsing.

recommendations.title: Missing append-only run_record and output_manifest
recommendations.problem: Unlike estimate-immune-score-analysis and gsva-analysis-and-visualization sibling skills, model-calibration-curve does not maintain run_record.txt or output_manifest.txt. session_info.txt is the only run record and it overwrites on each run.
recommendations.root_cause: The recording.R module present in estimate and GSVA skills was not included in the calibration curve skill. Only 4 modules vs 7-9 in sibling skills.
recommendations.fix: Add recording.R module and call write_run_record and write_output_manifest at end of run_analysis(). Include bootstrap_reps, years, seed, runtime, and output file list in each run record entry.

recommendations.title: Missing privacy advisory for clinical CSV input
recommendations.problem: Clinical CSV files for calibration analysis are likely to contain patient-level survival data. No anonymization advisory is present, unlike the GSVA sibling skill.
recommendations.root_cause: Privacy note added to GSVA skill was not included in model-calibration-curve.
recommendations.fix: Add to Input Validation section: 'If your clinical CSV can be linked to patients or protected records, anonymize it before use. This workflow writes calibration objects, statistics, and session metadata to the local output_dir.'

recommendations.title: Lighter module structure than sibling skills
recommendations.problem: With only 4 R modules (main, functions, run_analysis, utils), io and validation logic are bundled into functions.R and run_analysis.R. This makes targeted modification harder than in the 6-9 module sibling skills.
recommendations.root_cause: Calibration curve skill was designed with a simpler architecture; the single-mode operation may have influenced the decision to keep fewer modules.
recommendations.fix: Consider extracting io.R (CSV reading, output file writing) and validation.R (sample count, event encoding checks) into separate modules to align with the nomogram-construction architecture pattern.
